steps.title: Apply the degree-to-radian conversion formula
steps.content:
To convert degrees to radians, multiply the degree measure by $$\frac{\pi}{180}$$.
For $60^{\circ}$:
$$60^\circ \times \frac{\pi}{180} = \frac{60\pi}{180}$$

steps.title: Simplify the fraction
steps.content:
Divide both numerator and denominator by 60:
$$\frac{60\pi}{180} = \frac{\pi}{3} = \frac{1}{3}\pi$$ This is the radian measure equivalent to $60^{\circ}$.
B is correct.
